slides.title: What is Linear Regression?
slides.content: Linear regression is a statistical method that models the relationship between a dependent variable and one or more independent variables. It is used to predict outcomes and find trends.

slides.title: Simple Linear Regression Model
slides.content: Simple linear regression involves one dependent variable and one independent variable. It assumes a linear relationship between the variables.

slides.title: Understanding the Parameters
slides.content: In the equation, \( \beta_0 \) is the intercept, \( \beta_1 \) is the slope coefficient, and \( \epsilon \) represents the error term.

visual_assets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
X = np.array([[1], [2], [3]])
y = np.array([2, 2.5, 3.5])
model = LinearRegression().fit(X, y)
print(model.coef_, model.intercept_)
visual_assets.caption: Python Code for Simple Linear Regression

slides.title: Example Dataset and Fitted Line
slides.content: Visualizing a simple dataset with the fitted regression line can help in understanding how linear regression works.

slides.title: Applications of Linear Regression
slides.content: Linear regression is widely used in various fields such as economics, biology, engineering, and social sciences for predicting trends and making forecasts.

slides.title: Real-world Application Example
slides.content: Suppose a company uses linear regression to forecast sales based on advertising spend.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: Linear regression is a fundamental statistical tool in predictive modeling. It provides insights into relationships between variables and is essential for data analysis.
